channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Russia cuts mobile internet in Moscow citing drone security concerns
item.description: Russia has begun rolling mobile internet shutdowns in Moscow and other cities.

item.title: Al-Qaeda-linked fighters storm Mali prison, block food supplies to Bamako
item.description: Fighters attack 'Africa's Alcatraz', which detains high-value prisoners, and disrupt crucial supply chains to capital.

item.title: Infected passengers evacuated from hantavirus cruise ship
item.description: Three passengers have been evacuated from a cruise ship off the coast of Cape Verde after an outbreak of hantavirus.

item.title: What does the BJP’s victory in West Bengal mean for India’s democracy?
item.description: These state elections could reshape national politics for 200 million Muslims.

item.title: Israeli strike kills colonel in Gaza police force, say Palestinian medics
item.description: At least 17 others wounded in a targeted attack on Naseem al-Kalazani's vehicle in western Khan Younis city.

item.title: Indiana, Ohio primaries draw midterm battle lines, reinforce Trump’s pull
item.description: Latest votes set up key Senate race, underscore Trump's continued influence over Republican Party.

item.title: Why are Pentagon officials talking about Iran’s ‘deadly dolphins’?
item.description: Soraya Lennie discusses false claims about Iran using marine life in warfare.

item.title: Shoot Palestinians, not settlers: Israeli general exposes double standard
item.description: Leaks from the head of the Israeli army in the West Bank reveal two-tier firing policies and endemic prejudice.

item.title: Iran ‘has attained an elevated international standing’ says FM
item.description: The Iranian foreign minister said Iran has ‘attained an elevated international standing’ during the war.

item.title: Israel bombs Beirut’s southern suburb as it targets Hezbollah commander
item.description: It was the first attack on Beirut's southern suburbs since a truce in the Israel-Hezbollah war went into force on April 17.

item.title: UN demands Israel immediately releases flotilla activists
item.description: The United Nations has called on Israel to “immediately” release two activists detained on a flotilla bound for Gaza.

item.title: It’s not cricket: Pakistan athletes allowed in India for multilateral sport
item.description: India's government to allow Pakistani athletes to visit but maintains a sports ban on bilateral events like cricket.

item.title: The anti-Semitism smear that ruined Corbyn’s Labour now targets the Greens
item.description: Zack Polanski must not repeat Jeremy Corbyn’s mistakes.

item.title: LIVE: Bayern Munich vs PSG – Champions League semifinal
item.description: Follow our live build-up with full team news coverage ahead of our text commentary stream of the second leg.

item.title: Amputee football grows in Rwanda, fostering unity and resilience
item.description: Amputee football builds trust and community in Rwanda, helping survivors overcome stigma and find a sense of belonging.

item.title: Four killed in post-election violence in India’s West Bengal
item.description: Unrest grips the key eastern state after Modi's Hindu nationalist BJP wins the state election for the first time.

item.title: Who is Zack Polanski, UK Greens leader and rising political star?
item.description: Polanski has been likened to New York's Zohran Mamdani for his popular blend of environmental and socialist policies.

item.title: CNN founder Ted Turner dead at 87
item.description: No cause of death disclosed for the US media mogul who revolutionised news broadcasting.

item.title: Pro-Palestine group protests outside Israeli real estate event in New York
item.description: Demonstration against event featuring properties for sale in occupied West Bank is the second protest in six months.

item.title: UN demands Israel release Gaza flotilla activists, investigate abuse claims
item.description: Israel must 'immediately and unconditionally' release Saif Abu Keshek and Thiago Avila, rights office spokesman says.

item.title: Follow the money: How far-right Reform UK built a global network
item.description: Reform UK’s rise is backed by individual donors, overseas trips and networks that contrast with its nationalist message.

item.title: Burkina Faso authorities accused of secretly detaining prominent journalist
item.description: RSF says investigation found Atiana Serge Oulon held at Ouagadougou villa, contradicting authorities' account.

item.title: Araghchi in Beijing: How China could shape the direction of the US-Iran war
item.description: Analysts say shared US and Chinese interests in reopening the Strait of Hormuz could create a path towards peace.

item.title: Russian drone hits kindergarten in Ukraine
item.description: A Russian drone strike has hit a kindergarten in Ukraine’s Sumy region.

item.title: Elderly man rescued from rubble after Israeli strike on Lebanon
item.description: An 80-year-old man has been pulled out from under the rubble of his house in the south.
